lore.name: Kahaan
lore.tagline: A prince bound by duty, driven by truth
lore.description: Born into the military elite, Kahaan views the world through his floating HUD monocle—a distinct electric-blue tick over his left eye. He carries the state's authority in the neural mag-holsters at his ribs. When assigned the [REDACTED] case, he expects a procedural cleanup. Instead, he finds a thread that threatens to unravel the Mesh itself.
lore.quote: Truth is just data waiting to be verified.

hotspots.label: HUD MONOCLE
hotspots.description: Neural-diode HUD lattice. Floats over the left eye.

hotspots.label: NEURAL MAG-HOLSTER
hotspots.description: Twin pistols controlled via neural implant.

lore.name: Rudra
lore.tagline: He led the Tribhuj the day the army outlawed it. He has been in exile ever since.
lore.description: The Mountain Shadow. Rudra was leading the Tribhuj on the morning the army's Year-Turn Decree declared every creed of choice illegal. He did not flee. He did not fight a war he could not win without civilian blood. He disbanded what the state could see of his order, went into the Nepal hills, and answered to no title but dada for twenty years. Then a bomber claimed his order's name — and a promise pulled him out of silence.
lore.quote: The mountain does not hate the wind. It simply endures.

hotspots.label: WORKING-IRON TRIDENT
hotspots.description: Battered, weight-forward. Tool first, weapon second.

lore.name: General Pratap
lore.tagline: The hand that built the Mesh.
lore.description: Forty-seven years of service, seven administrations, zero ceded. The Smiling Architect of Order — regulation-neat chevron moustache, the Director's Signet on his right hand — is the iron spine that held Bharatvarsh together after the Republic failed. The Mesh is his design. The Quieting is his legacy. The newspapers call him the last unbroken promise of the 1985 Decree.
lore.quote: Chaos is a choice. Order is a responsibility.

hotspots.label: DIRECTOR'S SIGNET
hotspots.description: Right hand. Ashoka-wheel and triangular wings engraved in brushed steel. Issued only to the Supreme Commander.

hotspots.label: CHEVRON MOUSTACHE
hotspots.description: Regulation-neat. Squared ends. Never crosses the lip line. Fine silver along the edge for age readability.

hotspots.label: NAVY OFFICER'S COAT
hotspots.description: Powder-blue shirt beneath an open navy great-coat. Large emblem centred on the back. Small emblem, left shoulder. Right shoulder text-only.

hotspots.label: COMMANDER'S OMNI-HANDLE
hotspots.description: Pen-length baton in the inner-coat sheath. Telescopes. A twist locks it into a 14cm punch-dagger. Reads as a stylus in public; a last-ditch blade in the war room.

lore.name: Hana
lore.tagline: The only officer who can tell Kahaan 'no' — and does.
lore.description: The Moral Lens. Ops-Two to Kahaan's Ops-One. Left-handed, chin-length wedge bob, Olympic-grade marksmanship earned at a firing range where she once stalked the dawn shift trying to break a record her war-hero father had set. She did. He said, 'Good grouping.' Comrades speak of 'two Hanas' — the one who returns fire in three-word sentences and the one who sneaks contraband storybooks to the quartermaster's children. Both are real. The mask is a timing device.

hotspots.label: WEDGE BOB
hotspots.description: Chin-length, side-swept fringe, tapered wedge at the nape. On duty she tucks the left side behind her ear so the scope reads clean.

hotspots.label: BRACE-COMM
hotspots.description: Left wrist. She is left-hand dominant. When it wakes it throws a small blue-white map pane across her forearm.

hotspots.label: FIELD-DRESSING KIT
hotspots.description: Compact, clean white. The 'moral first' prop — her hand reaches it before the holster, even under pressure.

hotspots.label: PRECISION RIFLE
hotspots.description: Matte charcoal body, mid-weight barrel, low-profile scope. Advanced but restrained — precision over aggression.

lore.name: Arshi
lore.tagline: Some names arrive before the body they belong to.
lore.description: Heir-apparent of the Akakpen while Queen Kaali lives. Raised inside the tribe's treaty hills — adopted daughter of Kaali under the law that made bloodline and choice legally identical. Cross-trained on sword, bow, mixed martial arts, and firearms; carries a compact pulse gun inside the waistband at four o'clock. Begins defiant. Ends authoritative. In early arc her chin lifts two degrees before she speaks; late arc she lets the stillness of her posture do the speaking for her.

hotspots.label: THE BLUE GAZE
hotspots.description: Deep cool blue #0F2749. Almond, slight inner fold. The Akakpen heir mark.

hotspots.label: AKAKPEN KNOT
hotspots.description: Sash-clasp brooch in formal wardrobe; 80mm reflective heat-press on the field cloak. Monochrome. Princess-scale only — never the queen's gold ring.

hotspots.label: PULSE GUN
hotspots.description: Matte graphite chassis, white ceramic inlays, amber status LEDs. Carried IWB at 4 o'clock. Default mode: STUN.

hotspots.label: ALPINE CLOAK
hotspots.description: White winter cloak with balaclava and reflective goggles parked on the crown. Charcoal soft-armor beneath. Mountain boots with integrated steel teeth.

lore.name: Indrapur HQ
lore.tagline: The seat of the Directorate. The city that sleeps under drones.
lore.description: Indrapur — formerly known as Indraprastha — is the national capital of Bharatvarsh and the top-floor home of the General Directorate. General Pratap runs the state from a glass-walled command aerie above the city, beneath a triangle of wall icons that never moves: a Chandragupta portrait, a live national map, a framed constitutional preamble. At night, the avenues fall silent and the curfew drones paint slow white cones over the empty boulevards. The curfew breaks at six AM like a rotten plank under a foot.
lore.quote: Indrapur slept under the exacting discipline of its curfew drones, whose slow, patient circuits painted the avenues with cones of white.

hotspots.label: TOP-FLOOR COMMAND AERIE
hotspots.description: Glass-walled office of General Pratap, raised above the city. A raised-platform chair. A triangle of framed icons on the wall: Chandragupta portrait, national map, constitutional preamble.

hotspots.label: THE DIRECTOR'S DESK
hotspots.description: The only surface from which the state actually issues orders. Pratap leans lightly against its edge; his right hand drifts to the signet on his index finger when he is pretending to consider something.

hotspots.label: THE WALL MAP
hotspots.description: A live national map. His index finger lands on it first, then on the preamble, then on the visitor. The order is fixed. The visitor learns to cross the distance to speak.

hotspots.label: CURFEW DRONE CORRIDOR
hotspots.description: Cones of white light, slow and patient, from 9 PM to 6 AM. No permit, no exit. The avenues below read as geometry until daybreak.

lore.name: Lakshmanpur
lore.tagline: The city where the chronicle’s first stage was set.
lore.description: Lakshmanpur is the capital city of Uttar Pradesh — a Northern Plains bloc stronghold with dense Mesh coverage, an annual festive fortnight in October, and the widest pedestrian arenas in the country. On the last Friday of the festive fortnight, the city’s open-air stages fill with families. On one such night, a woman steps up to a microphone, speaks the word Tribhuj out loud for the first time in almost half a century, and detonates everything about what the country thought it had settled. Everything the chronicle investigates begins here.
lore.quote: Lakshmanpur is the outlier for theatrics — stage, pamphlets, all the shouting about ‘Tribhuj’.

hotspots.label: THE ARENA STAGE
hotspots.description: The open-air stage of the annual festive fortnight. A microphone. A voice that does not match the programme. A word that has been a crime since 1984.

hotspots.label: THE OVERPASS
hotspots.description: A hand-cart embedded in the side of a van as if the city had tried to absorb the blow with an organ it could spare. Lights running their program across a scene the program no longer applied to.

hotspots.label: THE FOOD STALL CRATER
hotspots.description: Where a food stall had been. Bowls turned into shrapnel. A picture in the first forensic file Kahaan opens.

hotspots.label: THE TRIAGE BAY
hotspots.description: A hospital receiving bay with its triage banners fluttering like flags who had lost their flagpoles. One of seven frames that make a single picture — coordination.

lore.name: Akakpen Tribe
lore.tagline: The might of the east. The gate that boots do not open.
lore.description: The Akakpen tribe of the eastern hills of Arunachal. A semi-sovereign people whose treaty-won pocket of autonomy is the only stretch of ground the Directorate does not walk freely on. They are governed not from a palace but from a council chamber with a round table, seven chairs, and a map of the hills inked by hand — ridge by ridge, river by river, annotated with names and jokes and warnings that exist nowhere else. Their perimeter is electric, their doctrine is non-lethal by design, and their treaty is the reason the gate is still a gate.
lore.quote: The non-lethal saved us. I'm here because you showed restraint.
lore.quoteAuthor: PRINCESS ARSHI, TO THE ROUND TABLE

hotspots.label: TIRED SAFFRON FLAG
hotspots.description: Flags a tired saffron fluttering off the porch rails of the capital cabins. Not green. Not deep-forest. The colour the Akakpen actually raise over their own roof.

hotspots.label: HAND-INKED HILL MAP
hotspots.description: Not the army's sterile satellite print. Ridge by ridge, river by river, inked by hand and annotated with names and jokes and warnings that existed nowhere else.

hotspots.label: NON-LETHAL DART
hotspots.description: A short leaf of feathered plastic. A small tube in the neck. The Akakpen pattern when they need men to stop without dying. Restraint by design.

hotspots.label: AKAKPEN KNOT
hotspots.description: Tribe insignia. Two nested mountain chevrons enclosing a hollow circle — mountain within mountain, life at the centre. Monochrome only. Worn at council carriage, never raised in place of the saffron.

lore.name: Bharatsena
lore.tagline: Polite inevitability. Order through control.
lore.description: The continental military state that has governed Bharatvarsh for almost half a century. Its apex command body, the Directorate, runs the nation through martial tribunals while parliament sits ceremonial in its own chamber. Curfew drones paint the metropoles' avenues at night with cones of white. Hovercams ride the crowds. Brace-Comms handle the payments. The state presents itself abroad as peace-through-strength; it enforces itself at home through a polite inevitability that makes compliance feel natural.
lore.quote: Human nature does not prosper without supervision.

hotspots.label: ASHOKA EMBLEM
hotspots.description: Twenty-four-spoke wheel framed by two triangular wings. Monochrome, bold black strokes on white, precise vertical symmetry. The mark on every coat, every banner, every signet ring.

hotspots.label: POWDER-BLUE / NAVY CODE
hotspots.description: Powder-blue shirt under open navy officer's over-coat. Large emblem centred on the back. Small emblem, left shoulder. Right shoulder text-only, plain block: the division call-sign.

hotspots.label: CURFEW DRONE
hotspots.description: Slow, patient circuits over the metropoles from nightfall to daybreak. Cones of white light. 9 PM to 6 AM. No permit, no exit.

hotspots.label: HOVERCAM OVERWATCH
hotspots.description: Drone nests at forty metres. Hovercams looped above crowd surfaces. Med-corridors threaded through the fences. The army's first-minute grammar during any incident.

lore.name: Bracecomm
lore.tagline: The Mesh in your sleeve. The rule in your veins.
lore.description: The standard wrist-band device of Bharatvarsh. Brace-Comms replaced smartphones years ago; they project 2D tiles and 3D holograms above the sleeve, carry encrypted comm channels, and handle biometric payments. Their face is “a little square of authority.” They pulse against the wrist for incoming bands, give a “tiny offended chirp” when muted, and obey a hard rule in stealth zones: transmit and you get triangulated; cry and you get found.
lore.specifications: Under-fabric sleeve nodes | 2D tile + 3D holo projection | Biometric-locked | Encrypted channels (public · patrol · private)
lore.quote: Transmit and you get triangulated; cry and you get found.

hotspots.label: THE TILE FACE
hotspots.description: “A little square of authority.” 2D interface for calls, maps, and payments. Tile sizes: 80×160mm for a call, 120×240mm for a map.

hotspots.label: BIOMETRIC WRIST STRIP
hotspots.description: Reads the wearer’s pulse, capillary pattern, and skin conductance. Unlocks on contact. Locks on removal. Cannot be borrowed.

hotspots.label: HOLO PROJECTOR
hotspots.description: 3D holograms along with the 2D interface — the capability that retired civilian smartphones. Lead innovator: Junaid Steels.

hotspots.label: MUTE SEAM
hotspots.description: The seam along the inner band. Slide it and the device gives a “tiny offended chirp” and stops transmitting. For stealth zones, drop it face-down in a drawer.

lore.name: Dart
lore.tagline: A short leaf of feathered plastic. A man on the ground.
lore.description: The Akakpen non-lethal dart. A small tube in the neck, a short leaf of feathered plastic still quivering lightly as if it was breathing. Standard perimeter issue across the eastern hills — because the tribe’s doctrine forbids lethal force between people who share the valleys. A Directorate regular who wakes up in the pine-bed with a bruise on his neck and no memory of the last forty minutes has probably met one.
lore.specifications: Compact tube · feathered plastic tail | Sleep-sap payload | Silent draw | Non-lethal first doctrine
lore.quote: The dart was one of theirs. Akakpen used the same pattern when they needed men to stop without dying.
lore.quoteAuthor: THE CHRONICLE, AT THE CRASH SITE

hotspots.label: FEATHERED TAIL
hotspots.description: “A short leaf of feathered plastic still quivering lightly as if it was breathing.” The tell for a scene the Akakpen have already walked through.

hotspots.label: PAYLOAD CHAMBER
hotspots.description: Sleep-sap. The exact agent is a treaty-held secret. The effect is not: an adult soldier drops in under two seconds and does not remember the fall.

hotspots.label: NECK TUBE
hotspots.description: The thin metal needle that delivers the payload. Aim point: lateral neck. Wake-up point: a pine-bed, several hours later, with a headache and no story.

hotspots.label: RED-CAP SAFETY
hotspots.description: Pull cap, arm dart. The red cap is the only thing between a non-lethal kit and an accident. Akakpen council rules it is the drill master’s first check, every morning.

lore.name: Oxy Pole
lore.tagline: Tall, engineered trees that hum like low vows.
lore.description: “Tall, engineered trees that fed districts with oxygen and quiet — they hummed like low vows.” Installed by the administration a couple of years before the chronicle begins. Their job is to convert carbon dioxide, scrub the air, and — quietly, in the same pole housings — carry the hovercam halos, the PA nodes, and the ambient sensors that make the Mesh breathable. Placards at their bases read: “Please stay away from these oxy-poles. Fiddling with them can affect their functionality.”
lore.specifications: Crown vanes · wash heads | Hovercam nest at peak | PA + environmental sensor housings | Harness-rigged for crown maintenance
lore.quote: Above, the oxy-poles — those tall, engineered trees that fed districts with oxygen and quiet — hummed like low vows.

hotspots.label: CROWN VANES
hotspots.description: The “engineered tree” canopy. Catalytic fronds. CO₂ in, O₂ out. The sound the manuscript names is the humming of these vanes under load.

hotspots.label: HOVERCAM NEST
hotspots.description: Docking halo for state hovercams. A pole is never just a pole. Every oxy-pole on every corner is also a perch.

hotspots.label: PA NODE
hotspots.description: Public-address housing. The voice the state uses when it wants to remind the street that the curfew begins at nine.

hotspots.label: BASE PLACARD
hotspots.description: “Please stay away from these oxy-poles. Fiddling with them can affect their functionality.” The only ground-level UI the public ever sees.

lore.name: Pulse Gun
lore.tagline: Carried inside the waistband. Default at STUN.
lore.description: The Akakpen princess-grade pulse gun. Matte graphite chassis, white ceramic inlays, tiny amber status LEDs. Compact enough to carry inside the waistband at four o’clock. Selector modes: STUN, DISRUPT, LETHAL. Default at STUN for palace carry — the first time the selector moves past that setting is not a scene the chronicle permits you to read before you’ve earned it. Princess Arshi carries one. A small Akakpen knot is engraved, monochrome and flat, on the grip panel.
lore.specifications: Matte graphite chassis · white ceramic inlays | Amber status LEDs | Three modes (STUN · DISRUPT · LETHAL) | IWB 4-o'clock carry | 12 mm knot engraved on grip panel

hotspots.label: GRAPHITE CHASSIS
hotspots.description: Matte graphite body. Low-snag, squared trigger guard, integral recoil sink. Built to vanish under a cloak or a council overdress.

hotspots.label: CERAMIC INLAY
hotspots.description: White ceramic inlays along the slide. The tribe’s single design flourish — functional too, they dissipate heat from the pulse coils.

hotspots.label: AMBER STATUS LED
hotspots.description: Tiny amber telltales along the aft-left of the frame. STUN holds steady; DISRUPT pulses; LETHAL stays lit. If an amber dot is steady, talk fast.

hotspots.label: MODE SELECTOR
hotspots.description: Aft-left toggle. Three positions. The first two are governance. The third is last-resort. Council rules mandate a default return-to-STUN after any field deployment.

lore.name: Surya
lore.tagline: The paper is clean. The world isn't.
lore.description: Major Surya carries no patronymic — unregistered at birth, raised at a range, handed a rifle before a surname rule existed to take. Lead of a classified Bharatsena strike cell called the Guhyakas. Warm-past missions for a handler he called mentor; cool-present memory of what those missions were actually for. He walked out of an interior detainment with a small metal tag in his pocket that he had been carrying, unknowing, for five years. His arc moves from Obedience to Conscience to Justice — earned in that order, without shortcut.
lore.quote: Evidence doesn't need a rank. It just needs daylight.

hotspots.label: RED ARMBAND
hotspots.description: Moral-state indicator. Starts the chronicle muted. Saturates the night he clears the desert perimeter and refuses to turn back.

hotspots.label: DARK BRACE-COMM
hotspots.description: Display runs dark permanently. Trust nothing connected.

hotspots.label: SUPPRESSED CARBINE
hotspots.description: Micro-rail, mid-weight, field-stripped daily. An off-record weapon for an off-record unit.

hotspots.label: ADAPTIVE CAMO
hotspots.description: Guhyakas issue. Pattern drifts with ambient palette. No insignia, no rank, no name tape.

lore.name: Kaali
lore.tagline: Stillness that issues orders without rising.
lore.description: Queen of the Akakpen. Late fifties, upright spine, the unhurried economy of a body that learned early that authority is in what you do not say. Long grey hair with white streaks gathered by a gold queen's hair-ring — the tribe's single piece of sovereign hardware. Taught the bow and the short single-edge sword to her own heir, Arshi, across mountain field seasons that never made the state's records. In the novel's present she is in an ICU bed, half her head under bandage, a single green eye doing the work of a throne. One old promise, whispered through an oxygen mask, is enough to pull a hermit out of twenty years of exile.
lore.quote: A queen does not command the mountain. She listens until the mountain speaks.

hotspots.label: EMERALD COMMAND EYE
hotspots.description: One green eye. Green, never blue. Steady, discerning, rarely widened — the Akakpen command line runs through it.

hotspots.label: GOLD HAIR-RING
hotspots.description: Antique gold, satin finish. The queen's sole insignia. Gathers grey-and-white hair into a sleek ponytail. Removed in the ICU; placed on the bedside tray.

hotspots.label: DIGNITY SHAWL
hotspots.description: A narrow dark-green shawl strip (#162B18) laid across the chest in ICU frames. Identity cue. Authority persists.

hotspots.label: OXYGEN MASK
hotspots.description: Clear, softly fogging on exhale. Recovery posture. Her voice through it is still the slowest one in the room.

lore.name: The Treaty Zone
lore.tagline: Where the Mesh ends and the treaty begins.
lore.description: The eastern hills of Arunachal — the semi-sovereign pocket won at the cost of decades of guerrilla stalemate and signed as the Treaty of the Eastern Hills. Ruled by a round table of seven chairs and a map of the hills inked by hand. Accessed through a gate whose perimeter grids will put a regular on the ground if he walks through without shield plates active. The Mesh halts at the treeline. The Directorate stops at the gate. Inside, the flags are a tired saffron and the council decides what happens next.
lore.quote: The Directorate does not fear the U.N., but they do fear optics across the hills.

hotspots.label: THE GATE · TRESPASS-GRIDS
hotspots.description: The Akakpen perimeter. Not ceremonial. These grids will put you on the ground if you walk through without shield plates active. Any Directorate regular who wants to parley stands at the mouth, carries shields, and talks first.

hotspots.label: THE ROUND TABLE
hotspots.description: The council chamber. Seven chairs. A room designed for listening. The only table in Bharatvarsh where the Directorate’s writ does not arrive ahead of the guest.

hotspots.label: THE HAND-INKED HILL MAP
hotspots.description: Not the army’s sterile satellite print. Ridge by ridge, river by river, inked by hand and annotated with names and jokes and warnings that existed nowhere else.

hotspots.label: TIRED SAFFRON FLAGS
hotspots.description: Wooden cabins raised slightly off the ground on stone piers. Shutters the colour of river mud. Flags a tired saffron fluttering off the porch rails. An administrative quarter that does not look like one from the outside.

lore.name: Mysuru
lore.tagline: The plateau city the chronicle rides into at golden hour.
lore.description: Mysuru sits on a granitic shelf where the sun hits harder and the secrets run deeper. Peninsular Command’s crown jewel — a city of basalt and laterite and dark glass, tri-script signage running English, Hindi and Telugu, engineers in civilian dress with military clearance. On the night of 20 October, fourteen synchronized detonations tear through its largest transit-mall. One of seven cities hit. The one where Kahaan sends his deputy, Rishaan, because the bomb placement looked like the work of a professional. It was.
lore.quote: It was an ode to the engineers that the building was still standing.

hotspots.label: JUNCTION MALL
hotspots.description: A five-storey transit-hub shopping mall. Fourteen synchronized detonations across multiple floors. Gaping holes across the facade with black soot smeared around them. The building was still standing.

hotspots.label: GRANITIC SHELF
hotspots.description: The plateau the city stands on. Tech-park terraces stepping down granite benches. Pergola-shaded transit forecourts. Matte ceramic plazas with basalt-inlay hover-lane chevrons.

hotspots.label: TRI-SCRIPT SIGNAGE
hotspots.description: English. Hindi. Telugu. Three scripts, three weights, every streetside sign. The only metropole in the chronicle that posts its notices in the southern state’s own letter-forms.

hotspots.label: ARMY AIR STRIP
hotspots.description: Just outside the city. Golden hour approach — dark grey at the bottom, bright orange at the top. Where Rishaan’s army jet lands the day after 20 October, into a staggered diamond escort and a matte charcoal Panthera waiting on the apron.

lore.name: Tribhuj
lore.tagline: Democracy died in 1984. Its ghost carries a trident.
lore.description: The Tribhuj is an ancient ascetic martial order that predates the dictatorship — not a modern reformation. Through the early 1980s, under Rudra's command, it became the loudest publicly-known voice for elected governance — peaceful, principled, and inconvenient enough to be declared treasonous alongside religion, caste, and surnames in one sweeping 1984 decree. After the ban, the symbols didn't die. They went to the walls. Grey tridents scratched into alley plaster at 3 a.m. Pamphlets folded into chapati wrappers during Festival Fortnight. Basement meetings where the word 'election' was spoken like a prayer. Rudra kept the order alive underground until the army's Teesta Treaty broke its popular support around 2003 — then it went dormant. Then, in October 2025, seven cities burned under a flag bearing the same trident — a separate, state-manufactured impostor brand calling itself the 'New Tribhuj.' Whether the original order and this new violence share anything beyond a symbol is the question that will tear Bharatvarsh apart.
lore.quote: The trident was never a weapon. It was a ballot.

lore.name: The Mesh
lore.tagline: Nobody remembers when they stopped noticing the hovercams.
lore.description: The Mesh is not a thing you can point to. It is the state of a city that has forgotten when it stopped noticing the hovercams. It is the curfew drones painting slow white cones across Indrapur from 9 PM to 6 AM, the oxy-poles humming on every corner, the wrist-scans at every kiosk, the Brace-Comms pulsing on every officer’s wrist. It ends at the treeline of Arunachal. Everywhere else, it is weather — ambient, total, and unremarkable.
lore.specifications: Curfew drones · hovercams · wrist-scan checkpoints · oxy-pole sensor halos | Brace-Comm routing layer | Ends at the treeline of Arunachal
lore.quote: Nobody remembers when they stopped noticing the hovercams.

hotspots.label: CURFEW DRONE
hotspots.description: Slow, patient circuits over the avenues of Indrapur from 9 PM to 6 AM. Cones of white light. The Mesh’s nightly voice.

hotspots.label: OXY-POLE HALO
hotspots.description: Every corner. Every valley floor inside the treeline. Cameras and PA and environmental sensors wrapped around each engineered tree like a dark scarf.

hotspots.label: WRIST-SCAN CHECKPOINT
hotspots.description: Biometric gate at the mouth of every controlled lane. Pulse, capillary, gait — all read in one step. Curfew violators receive instant fines through the same reader.

hotspots.label: TREELINE EDGE
hotspots.description: The hard boundary. Everywhere the Mesh reaches, order is weather. Everywhere it does not, the Akakpen gate decides what happens next.

lore.name: Neural Diodes
lore.tagline: The faint surgical scallop behind his left ear.
lore.description: Major Kahaan’s battlefield augmentation. A faint surgical scallop is hidden behind his left ear — the external mark of a sub-dermal diode lattice grafted during the African relief detail in 2021, under shellfire, as a last resort. The diodes let him float twin pistols at shoulder height on faint electric-blue shimmer threads, pilot a scout motorcycle by intent, and host readouts on a HUD that answers directly to his gaze. It is a masterpiece. It is also slowly breaking him. An EMP knocks out everything it touches. Migraines arrive without warning. A nose bleeds in briefings. In the quiet hours he wonders whether the machine is a tool he uses or a system that is using him.
lore.specifications: Sub-dermal lattice | Surgical scallop behind LEFT ear | Mag-holster range ~2m | EMP-vulnerable | Side effects: migraines, tinnitus, transient aphasia
lore.quote: He touched the faint surgical scallop hidden behind his left ear — the place where flesh had agreed to host a decision.

hotspots.label: SURGICAL SCALLOP
hotspots.description: The faint curved scar behind Kahaan’s LEFT ear. Small enough to miss. The only external trace of the lattice buried under the skin of his skull.

hotspots.label: SHIMMER THREADS
hotspots.description: Faint electric-blue filaments that hover between his shoulder rig and his hands. The visible sign that the mag-holsters have woken up and the pistols are no longer under anyone’s thumb.

hotspots.label: MAG-HOLSTER PISTOL
hotspots.description: One of twin sidearms that detach from the shoulder rig and float at shoulder height. Thought-pointed, thought-fired. Range of the lattice: about two metres off the body.

hotspots.label: DERMAL LATTICE
hotspots.description: A web of thin sub-dermal filaments that runs from the scallop down the neck to the shoulder rig. Lights up faintly under neon when the host is tense. Never supposed to be visible, and always a little bit visible anyway.

lore.name: HUD Monocle
lore.tagline: Wind speed: 0.5 km/h, NW. Elevation: −4°. Range: 187 m.
lore.description: The HUD — heads-up display, standard issue to Bharatvarsh officers in helmets and visors, augmented to an apparatus-less floating lens for those few whose neural-diode lattice can carry it. Manuscript-grade helmets paint icons across the visor like faint stars: on the right, a call glyph and a map slice; on the left, engine health, gear, fuel, a small pulsing dot that means nothing to anyone who isn’t the wearer. A tap on the visor’s temple throws ghost vectors into the air for only the host to see. A sniper at his kneel reads wind speed and range off the top right without ever lifting his eye.
lore.specifications: Helmet / visor overlay (standard) | Apparatus-less floating lens (augmented officers only) | Electric-blue glyph rendering | Temple-tap + gaze-intent input
lore.quote: Wind speed: 0.5 km/h, NW. Elevation: −4°. Range: 187 m.

hotspots.label: FLOATING LENS
hotspots.description: The blue-tick overlay — in the augmented variant, it sits in the air in front of the left eye with no visible mount. In the standard variant, it’s painted onto the inside of a helmet visor.

hotspots.label: GLYPH STREAM
hotspots.description: Scrolling tactical data — call glyph, map slice, engine health, wind, elevation, range. The glyphs are deliberately illegible to onlookers; they render for the host only.

hotspots.label: TEMPLE-TAP POINT
hotspots.description: Tap the visor’s temple and “the helmet obligingly puts little ghost vectors into the air for only him to see. Green lines fan, intersect, remember gravity.”

hotspots.label: WAYPOINT DOT
hotspots.description: “A small pulsing dot that meant nothing to anyone who wasn’t him.” Personal waypoint — an aim-point the HUD reserves for its host.
